occurrences.line_text: | done | Finish refactor sealing verification and report CLI dispatch fix | Codex | Fixed the final Event Alpha command-registry mismatch where impact-hypothesis and incident reports still read stale `args.include_api`; parser-backed tests now cover `--include-legacy` dispatch. Full sealing verification passed: standalone `760/760`, safe pytest `777 passed`, compileall, old-import/shim/size/class/final reports, integrated radar doctor, export smoke (`bad_entries=0`), Catalyst-frame e2e, and `make verify`. |
occurrences.reason: old CLI/Make aliases remain supported while canonical historical aliases exist

occurrences.line_text: | done | Harden ops CLI regression gate | Codex | Restored wrong-depth relative imports in the legacy RSI CLI service, added subprocess smokes for `--status`/backup/restore/log rotation plus static relative-import integrity coverage, added `pytest>=8.0`, and made `make verify` depend on hard-failing `make test-full` so the pytest-compatible package suite cannot be silently skipped. |

occurrences.line_text: | done | Harden live-provider readiness telemetry and stale namespace handling | — | Integrated radar smoke now writes no-call live-provider activation readiness JSON/Markdown before source coverage, source coverage/daily brief link readiness artifacts or print the readiness command, research-review delivery rows carry rendered/eligible/skipped counts plus family-level skipped-candidate summaries, and stale namespaces can be explicitly marked so strict doctor ignores superseded artifacts unless legacy inspection is requested. |

occurrences.line_text: | done | Fix CryptoPanic Growth Weekly request model | — | CryptoPanic live fetching now defaults to `https://cryptopanic.com/api/growth_weekly/v2/posts/`, reads the canonical `RSI_EVENT_DISCOVERY_CRYPTOPANIC_API_TOKEN` with legacy aliases, sends only Growth-supported params (`auth_token`, `public`/`following`, `currencies`, `regions`, `filter`, `kind`, `page`), batches validated currency queries, omits Growth-unsupported `search`/`size`/pull/content params, records a redacted request ledger, enforces weekly/run/day quota caps, and surfaces quota/unsupported-param/token-leak checks in preflight, source coverage, run ledger, and artifact doctor. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make Event Alpha delivery ledger status explicit | — | Fresh notification delivery rows now persist explicit `delivery_mode`, `delivery_state`, `status_detail`, send-guard, would-send, sent, and failed fields in addition to legacy `state`. Strict artifact doctor and send-readiness block fresh latest-run rows with missing/contradictory delivery status, while old rows remain readable. The notification inbox has a compact `--event-alpha-burn-in-review` mode for phone-friendly review, and the deep no-send rehearsal now has an explicit deterministic fixture-candidate target plus tighter fast-rehearsal bounds. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Finish notify_llm_deep rehearsal send-readiness gate | — | Heartbeat/no-digest notification previews now source completed/raw-event/extraction/core-opportunity/alertable/provider/LLM counts from the latest run ledger and canonical core store instead of rendering stale zero summaries. Delivery rows persist portable `notification_preview_relpath` metadata, and readiness resolves preview paths by relpath, namespace default, then legacy absolute fallback so stale `/Users/...` paths do not block another checkout. Blocked/would-send rows distinguish no-send rehearsal, send-guard, quality, cooldown, and not-due states. `make event-alpha-send-readiness PROFILE=notify_llm_deep_rehearsal` is the final artifact gate before enabling Telegram: it requires a completed latest run, strict artifact doctor, matching preview summary, canonical core delivery identity, live-confirmed alertable items, clear send-guard status, resolvable preview path, and Telegram readiness for real sends. A capped `make event-alpha-notify-llm-deep-real-no-send-rehearsal-fast` target exists for quicker preview/readiness proof. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Canonicalize Event Alpha notification identity | — | Routed Telegram notifications now reconcile through canonical CoreOpportunity rows before delivery, key delivery ledgers by `agg:...` core ids, preserve lower-level source alert ids as metadata, write multi-lane local notification previews plus no-candidate rehearsal previews, and block rejected-only/no-result/skipped-budget live digest items unless another real confirmation source exists. Artifact doctor now blocks current digest/escalation delivery rows missing canonical core id, feedback target, canonical card path, or canonical alert id, while latest-run scoped delivery checks label stale pre-core rows as legacy diagnostics unless an all-row sweep is requested. Broad BTC/ETH/SOL Strategy/MSTR treasury-valuation context is capped below digest without accepted/direct/fresh confirmation. Telegram digest copy is compact and hides raw debug fields/full local paths; fixture/no-send smokes exercise VELVET accepted high-priority, AAVE accepted digest, and BTC/TAO rejected controls, and `make event-alpha-notify-llm-deep-real-no-send-rehearsal` proves the actual deep profile with Telegram sends guarded off. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Polish Event Alpha traceability and live readiness | — | Research cards now carry current run/profile/namespace lineage plus incident, hypothesis, watchlist, core opportunity, alert/snapshot/card, and source ids; legacy lineage gaps are explicit. Opportunity audit, inbox, and feedback commands now agree on core opportunity / hypothesis / incident / alert / snapshot / card / watchlist targets. Daily briefs and quality reviews show a canonical operator-view note plus market freshness readiness, profile/status output explains frame-enabled `notify_llm_quality` behavior, and new `event-alpha-feedback-readiness` plus no-send live-style frame smoke targets check handoff readiness. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Clean Event Alpha operator-facing output | — | Daily briefs now lead with executive summary plus one-shot core-opportunity sections for high-priority, validated digest, watchlist, local/capped, canonical incidents, and system health. Already-promoted opportunities are filtered out of exploratory digest/near-miss views, near-miss rows are de-duplicated by incident/asset/role/impact path, quality review only lists explicit false-positive suspicion reasons, card indexes group core/near-miss/local/diagnostic/legacy cards, and inbox wording reflects core opportunity review. |

occurrences.line_text: | done | Polish catalyst-frame live-style artifacts and operator reports | — | Added a no-send `notify_llm_quality_frame` profile and `make event-alpha-notify-llm-quality-frame-smoke` so the `notify_llm_quality` artifact shape can be proved with fixture catalyst frames, run-ledger counters, incidents, hypotheses, daily brief, quality review, cards, audit metadata, and strict doctor checks without live providers or sends. Catalyst-frame skip reasons are normalized for disabled/missing-key/budget/no-selection/deadline cases, run-ledger legacy nulls read as zero/dicts, daily briefs show the selected namespace, and quality review de-duplicates operator-facing core opportunities while preserving supporting categories and impact paths for audit. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Harden quality lifecycle caps and incident subjects | — | Watchlist lifecycle caps now apply to non-hypothesis rows with bad quality fields as well as hypothesis-derived rows: local-only/exploratory, zero-score, insufficient impact/evidence/source, or `candidate_role=unknown_with_reason` rows cannot safely remain active watchlist/high-priority unless they persist an explicit non-active final state and cap reason. Stale persisted final states are recomputed when quality fields exist, and missing-quality fresh alert/playbook rows receive conservative local-only defaults. Artifact doctor now reports universal, hypothesis, non-hypothesis, capped, uncapped, legacy, diagnostic, invalid, and garbage-subject incident counts. Incident subject validation rejects boilerplate/prose/SEO fragments such as `Actions`, `Announcements`, `However`, `It`, `LLM`, `Non`, `Note`, `Only`, `Best Prediction Market Apps`, and `Polymarket Invite Code SBWIRE`; `Polymarket World Cup Volume` normalizes to `World Cup` when sports context exists. Existing garbage-subject artifacts are quarantined at read/report time and hidden unless diagnostics are explicitly requested. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make canonical incidents the Event Alpha state spine | — | Incident identity now propagates as top-level `incident_*` aliases across impact hypotheses, hypothesis-store rows, hypothesis-derived watchlist rows, alert snapshots, run-ledger rows, incident reports, and artifact doctor checks. Hypothesis watchlist keys use `incident_id + asset/sector identity + candidate_role + impact_path_type` when available, so duplicate source updates enrich the same incident/watchlist row instead of fragmenting state. Material update reasons now include incident-specific independent-source, cause-status, claim, market-reaction, causal-mechanism, and role-change reasons. Strict artifact doctor checks block fresh hypothesis/watchlist/alert rows missing required incident ids while warning on legacy rows. |
occurrences.reason: artifact compatibility field or historical/default row semantics

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make Event Alpha quality verdicts cap watchlist lifecycle state | — | Final opportunity-quality verdicts now cap lifecycle state before active-watchlist, monitor, router, alert snapshot, daily brief, inbox, and research-card consumers see rows. `local_only`, insufficient-data, and zero-score rows cannot remain active `WATCHLIST`/`HIGH_PRIORITY`; they persist as `QUALITY_BLOCKED`/local review rows with requested/final state and a `quality_state_block_reason`. Valid `watchlist`/`high_priority` verdicts still allow active watchlist states, and `EVENT_PASSED`/`ARMED` lifecycle progression remains possible for validated watchlist-quality rows. Daily briefs and quality review show `Quality-Capped Watchlist Rows` separately; artifact doctor strict mode blocks fresh uncapped state conflicts and warns on legacy conflicts. Block reasons now describe missing evidence rather than positive evidence, and candidate-discovery funnel metrics distinguish raw terms from candidate-like/accepted/context-validated/promoted candidates. `TRIGGERED_FADE_RESEARCH` remains preserved only for deterministic `event_fade.py` + `proxy_fade`. |
occurrences.reason: documentation mention classified as historical or compatibility context

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make Event Alpha routing obey final quality verdicts | — | Router decisions now apply a final opportunity-quality gate before operator-facing research routes. `local_only`, `exploratory`, insufficient-data/source-noise/ticker-collision, and zero-score verdicts are downgraded to local/store-only output even if the older watchlist/playbook path requested digest/watchlist/high-priority routing. Alert snapshots now persist canonical requested route/tier, final route/tier after quality gate, alertable-after-quality-gate, block reason, and snapshot quality classification (`current_clean`, `quality_gated_local`, `legacy_conflict`, `missing_final_route`, `stale_pre_quality_gate`), while preserving requested/legacy route values for audit. Notification plans, routed Telegram copy, inbox queues, daily briefs, cards, quality review, policy simulator, and artifact doctor use the final route by default. Quality-gated local-only rows and legacy conflicts are separated from would-send/delivered review queues; fresh conflicts or missing final routes block strict doctor checks, with strict-legacy available for migration audits. Candidate-discovery funnel and quality-tuning reports now distinguish raw terms, resolver attempts, accepted/rejected candidates, context validation, promotion, near-threshold candidates, weak patterns, and blocker sources. `TRIGGERED_FADE_RESEARCH` remains preserved only for deterministic `event_fade.py` + `proxy_fade`. |
occurrences.reason: artifact compatibility field or historical/default row semantics

occurrences.line_text: | done | Canonicalize Event Alpha top-level quality artifacts | — | Fresh hypothesis, watchlist, and alert snapshot rows now receive canonical top-level signal-quality fields even when context is missing: conservative local-only/insufficient-data defaults, explicit `why_local_only`/`why_not_watchlist`, manual verification items, and persisted upgrade/downgrade diagnostics. Artifact doctor strict mode now checks top-level fields directly and reports fresh hypothesis/watchlist/alert missing counts separately from legacy rows, so nested `score_components` can no longer mask bad fresh artifacts. Quality review/audit/policy simulation now show quality source/coverage, named policy scenarios, and richer candidate-discovery funnel diagnostics. Signal-quality fixtures expanded to 21 cases including political meme proxy, CryptoPanic tag, URL-only false positive, candidate-discovery, and Hyperliquid/HYPE identity cases. |
occurrences.reason: artifact compatibility field or historical/default row semantics

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make Event Impact Hypothesis artifacts latest-run diagnosable | — | `event_impact_hypotheses.jsonl` reports can now filter by latest run, explicit run id, or since timestamp, while summarizing total/latest/historical/legacy row counts and schema gaps. Stored hypotheses carry generated vs executed query details, rejected validation samples include `result_score`, daily briefs show rejected evidence reason/title samples, reports audit suspicious external entities parsed as crypto candidates, and `event-alpha-notify-llm-deep-scheduled` runs the bounded `notify_llm_deep` profile under the same guarded notification path. This is reporting/observability only and does not change promotion, routing, normal RSI rows, paper/live writes, trading, or `TRIGGERED_FADE` eligibility. |

occurrences.line_text: | done | Make Event Alpha artifacts profile-clean | — | Added `event_alpha_artifacts.py` for profile/run-mode/namespace path context, `event_alpha_artifact_doctor.py` for artifact lineage checks, run-ledger and alert-snapshot lineage fields, test/fixture/replay and legacy/default filtering in burn-in/readiness/health/brief/explain checks, snapshot availability classification, strict doctor mode, manifest/doctor data in burn-in packs, and artifact lineage in research cards. Profiled Make targets and artifact-backed reports now resolve namespaced paths from the requested profile before loading rows, disclose profile/namespace/run-mode/run-ledger/alert-store context, and `--event-alpha-preflight` checks profile paths, provider readiness, LLM budget/key state, and send guards before daily burn-in. Legacy rows still require an explicit migration flag. Everything remains research-only and cannot send, trade, paper trade, write normal RSI signal rows, or create `TRIGGERED_FADE`. |
